top_signals.title: UK Factory Growth Spurt Set to Be Short-Lived, PMI Shows
top_signals.summary: UK factories grew at the fastest pace in four years as the industry sought to get ahead of price increases and supply strains triggered by the Middle East conflict, a key survey found.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: French Navy Intercepts Suspected Shadow Fleet Vessel Bound From Russia
top_signals.summary: The French Navy has boarded and seized in the Atlantic a Russia-linked oil tanker which is under international sanctions, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Monday. The French navy on Sunday morning seized the Tagor, an oil tanker that departed from Russia, Macron wrote on in a post on X today. The operation of the boarding and seizure was carried out in the high seas in the Atlantic with the support of France’s allies, including the UK, the French President said.   France is constantly seeking to prevent shadow fleet vessels from…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UK manufacturing survives Iran war impact
top_signals.summary: The UK’s manufacturing sector appeared to survive the immediate impact of the Iran war in May, new data has suggested, as researchers suggested that production rose to a four-year high.  S&P Global’s purchasing managers’ index (PMI) for the sector hit 53.9 in May, well above the neutrality figure of 50 and higher than April’s recording [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
